欢迎收看火箭报道3.15版！小升降机的领域在本版中充斥着新闻。像往常一样，我们的报道报道了来自世界各地的新闻，本周从德国到中国，再到印度，再到南美。我们是一家全球性的企业。
一如既往，我们欢迎读者提交，如果您不想错过任何一期，请使用下面的框进行订阅(表单将不会显示在启用AMP的网站版本上)。每一份报告都将包括关于小型、中型和重型火箭的信息，以及日历上未来三次发射的快速展望。
火箭实验室发射光子卫星。发射公司表示，它已经将第一颗内部设计和建造的运行卫星送入轨道。第一道光被部署到轨道上执行火箭实验室的第14次电子任务，我真不敢相信它不是光学的，它于8月31日从新西兰的发射综合体1号发射升空。任务的主要客户是卡佩拉空间公司的一颗100公斤重的微型卫星。
不再只是一家火箭公司了。一份新闻稿称，Photon的发射巩固了该公司从一家发射提供商演变为一家提供全包式卫星和航天器组件、发射和在轨服务的端到端空间解决方案公司的发展。光子的设计目的是为卫星提供动力和推进等太空服务，甚至允许向月球和金星执行深空任务。(Ken the Bin提交)。
维珍银河确定下一次试飞日期。据CNBC报道，维珍银河公司提交给联邦通信委员会的文件显示，该公司计划在10月22日进行下一次载人航天测试。这次飞行很可能是太空旅游公司计划完成的两次太空飞船系统测试中的第一次，而且船上应该只有两名试飞员。
商业服务，终于，明年？…。维珍银河上个月表示，第二次测试太空飞行的日期尚未确定，机舱内将有四名任务专家。如果两次试飞都成功，维珍银河预计创始人理查德·布兰森爵士将在2021年第一季度飞行。这次里程碑式的飞行将标志着该公司商业旅游服务的开始。(Ken the Bin，JohnCarter17和DanNeely提交)。
跟上埃里克·伯杰的空间报道最简单的方法是注册他的时事通讯，我们会在你的收件箱里收集他的故事。
LandSpace筹集了1.75亿美元。据SpaceNews报道，中国发射公司LandSpace在C+系列融资中筹集了1.75亿美元，用于开发其朱雀-2系列甲烷/液氧运载火箭。两周前，中国竞争对手发射公司ispace获得了1.73亿美元的B系列融资。这两个都是令人印象深刻的现金转移，表明了严肃的意图。
一枚强大的小型火箭。LandSpace正在为2021年6月朱雀2号的首次发射而努力。根据LandSpace的说法，高49.5米，两级的朱雀2号将能够将4000公斤送入200公里的近地轨道或2000公斤到500公斤的太阳同步轨道。(由platykurtic和Ken the Bin提交)。
美国宇航局探空火箭发射尘埃2号任务。周二，一枚二级黑布兰特九号亚轨道探空火箭从新墨西哥州的白沙导弹靶场发射，搭载着尘埃-2科学任务。NASA表示，火箭将有效载荷发射到约346公里的远地点，然后通过降落伞返回地球。
廉价获得微重力。尘埃2的目标是研究垂死的恒星和超新星抛出的单个原子是如何粘在一起的。当它们这样做时，原子就会形成尘埃颗粒--这是我们宇宙的一些基本构件。美国宇航局戈达德太空飞行中心的行星科学家、首席研究员乔·努斯说，我们正在努力做的是复制在至少两个天体物理环境中发生的事情。(JohnCarter17提交)。
蓝色起源兽医开办了几家新公司。据“宇宙日志”报道，Blue Origin本周迎来了20岁生日，虽然这家私人持股公司还没有把人送入太空，也没有把火箭送入轨道，但它已经催生了新一代太空初创公司。相对论空间是相当知名的，但目前有更多的公司正在从半隐形模式中脱颖而出。
发射和太空推进。其中之一，斯托克空间技术公司，似乎正在研究能够重复使用上层级的技术。REACH太空技术公司似乎是一家推进公司。海星空间公司表示，它正在开发一种按需的太空运输服务。我们将把所有这些公司都加入我们的追踪公司名单中！(Ken the Bin和BH提交)。
巴西发射场评估提案。巴西航天局表示，他们已经开始评估11家有兴趣从位于该国北大西洋海岸的阿尔坎塔拉航天中心发射火箭的公司提交的11份建议书。该地点位于赤道以南仅2度，为执行赤道任务提供了绝佳的领土。
转向商业用途。据Paralloic Arc报道，经过初步分析，两家公司将在10月30日之前敲定他们的提案。目前尚不清楚巴西航天港将接受多少公司，但似乎可能会优先考虑拥有成熟或接近成熟的火箭设计的公司。到目前为止，这个太空港主要用于军事发射。(Ken the Bin提交)。
澳大利亚火箭公司签署澳元有效载荷。吉尔穆尔空间技术公司表示，它已经与澳大利亚客户签订了不早于2022年首次发射厄里斯火箭的合同。太空机器公司已经签约将一艘重达35公斤的航天器发射到轨道上。Gilmour Space的联合创始人兼首席执行官亚当·吉尔穆尔(Adam Gilmour)表示，这很可能是第一个从澳大利亚发射场用澳大利亚火箭发射到轨道上的澳大利亚有效载荷。
在这个十年的中期谈论一场盛大的比赛...。第一批Eris火箭被宣传为能够将305公斤的有效载荷发射到近地轨道，将215公斤的有效载荷发射到500公里的太阳同步轨道。该公司表示，其目标是到2025年达到每年12次发射的飞行速度，并帮助刺激澳大利亚航天产业的拓宽。(Ken the Bin和platykurtic提交)。
德国考虑北海发射设施。德国政府正在研究德国工业界提出的在北海建造卫星移动发射台的建议。根据该计划，重达一吨的小型卫星将用德国制造的火箭发射。据英国广播公司报道，德国工业联合会正在为该项目进行游说。
极地发射的好地方。人民民主联盟将是公私合作伙伴关系。德国媒体援引BDI提案的话说，德国发射台在技术上是可行的，在战略和经济上都有意义。可能从该平台发射的公司中有伊萨航空航天公司(Isar AerSpace)。(由cush ack，Ken the Bin和JohnCarter17提交)。
中国发射了一架神秘的航天飞机。据SpaceNews报道，经过几个月的低调准备，中国在酒泉卫星发射中心于周五发射了一艘可重复使用的实验性航天器。长征二号F运载火箭在发射后将航天器送入轨道，时间不详。
也许是X-37B的克隆？……经过一段时间的在轨运行后，飞船将返回预定的中国着陆点。新华社在发射后的报道中称，它将在飞行过程中测试可重复使用的技术，为和平利用太空提供技术支持。飞机在太空飞行了大约两天后降落。这条由NPR&39；的杰夫·布鲁姆菲尔撰写的推特帖子提供了关于任务落地的更多有趣的细节。(Ken the Bin提交)。
月船3号登月任务可能于明年发射。据DNA India报道，印度的登月任务可能会在2021年第一季度发射。去年9月月船2号任务失败后，印度航天局表示将再次尝试在月球表面软着陆。
进入Chandrayaan-3..。由于与月船二号任务一起发射的轨道器仍在运行，所以这次后续发射将只携带一个着陆器。预计它将搭载GSLV Mark III火箭从印度的萨蒂什·达万航天中心发射，印度将试图成为第四个在月球上软着陆的国家。(JohnCarter17提交)。
中国仍然存在有毒火箭问题。周一，一枚长征4B火箭搭载着一颗遥感卫星从中国太原卫星发射中心发射升空。这个有50年历史的航天港位于中国中北部，距北京西南约500公里。就像中国火箭从内陆太原设施发射的第一级经常发生的那样，用过的长征4B助推器落在了航天港的下游。在这种情况下，它降落在一所学校附近，不出所料地产生了大量的有毒气体云。
生活在肼的梦(或噩梦)中。据Ars报道，作为运载火箭燃料的联氨在世界上大部分地区已经被逐步淘汰。美国最后一枚使用肼的主要火箭是联合发射联盟的Delta II火箭，它在第二级使用了这种有毒燃料。然而，中国的大部分发射舰队都是由肼燃料和四氧化二氮提供动力的。这包括其人类额定的长征2F火箭，以及广泛使用的长征4号系列。
德尔塔4号重型火箭将于9月18日重启发射。据“今日佛罗里达报”报道，联合发射联盟团队已经确定了上月底德尔塔4号重型火箭戏剧性地在最后一秒中止的原因。这为不早于周五一周的另一次尝试奠定了基础，确切时间尚未公布。这将是第三次尝试发射NROL-44机密任务。
打开调节器..。加州大学洛杉矶分校(ULA)首席执行官托里·布鲁诺周三通过推特表示，8月29日，卡纳维拉尔角空军基地37号发射综合体的三个压力调节器中的一个压力调节器的隔膜撕裂，导致在起飞前三秒进行了电脑控制的擦洗。发动机短暂着火，但火箭仍牢牢地停留在垫子上。(BH和JohnCarter17提交)。
军方对超重升降机采取观望态度。美国军方对联合发射联盟(United Launch Alliance)和太空探索技术公司(SpaceX)目前提供的产品感到满意，但它正在密切关注未来正在开发的超重型火箭。这是根据美国太空部队(SpaceNews)负责发射服务采购的D·杰森·科瑟恩准将(Brig.Gen.D.Jason Cothern)本周发表的评论(SpaceNews报道了他的评论)。
如果你建造了它，他们会来吗？……我们相信，目前的供应商满足了我们目前在不久的将来的计划。这是科恩将军在回答一位观众关于“星际飞船”和“新格伦”等超重型运载火箭潜在军事价值的问题时说的，这些运载火箭正在开发中，可以飞向月球和更远的地方。这是科恩将军在回答一位观众关于“星际飞船”和“新格伦”等超重型运载火箭潜在军事价值的问题时所说的话。至于下一代运载火箭可能需要什么，作为军事太空的主要采购商，这是一个我们非常关心的问题，科瑟恩说。(JohnCarter17和platykurtic提交)。
SpaceX本周可能会将一个测试坦克滚动到垫子上。据NASASpaceflight.com报道，SpaceX‘s Boca Chica设施的下一次测试可能会使用测试坦克，而不是全尺寸的星际飞船原型。这是一个比它的前身SN7更大的测试箱，由304L系列不锈钢(或至少是该合金的变种)制成。它很可能会被推到爆发点，这样SpaceX的工程师就可以了解它的极限。
准备再来一次流行音乐..。虽然304L可能不是SpaceX希望在更长远的将来用于星际飞船和超重飞船的最终合金，但之前所有的星际飞船都是由301系列合金制成的。从SN8开始的未来版本将使用这种新合金。由于有两个发射架可供使用，SN7.1是否会在星际飞船的常规座架上驻扎，还是第二个测试座架，还有待观察。(Platykurtic提交)。
九月份。11：00火箭3.1|阿斯特拉演示任务|阿拉斯加科迪亚克航天港|世界协调时02：00
note_en:
Welcome to Edition 3.15 of the Rocket Report! The realm of small lift overflows with news in this edition. And as usual, our report covers news from around the world, spanning this week from Germany, to China, to India, to South America. Ours is a global enterprise.
 As always, we  welcome reader submissions, and if you don't want to miss an issue, please subscribe using the box below (the form will not appear on AMP-enabled versions of the site). Each report will include information on small-, medium-, and heavy-lift rockets as well as a quick look ahead at the next three launches on the calendar.
  Rocket Lab launches a Photon satellite. The launch company  said it has sent its first in-house-designed and -built operational satellite into orbit. "First Light" was deployed to orbit on Rocket Lab's 14th Electron mission, "I Can't Believe It's Not Optical," which lifted off from Launch Complex 1 in New Zealand on August 31. The mission's primary customer was a 100kg microsatellite for Capella Space.
 Not just a rocket company anymore ... Photon's launch cements the company's "evolution from a launch provider to an end-to-end space solutions company that offers turnkey satellites and spacecraft components, launch, and on-orbit," a  news release said. Photon is designed to provide in-space services such as power and propulsion to satellites, even allowing for deep-space missions to the Moon and Venus. (submitted by Ken the Bin)
 Virgin Galactic sets date for next test flight. Virgin Galactic plans to conduct its next crewed spaceflight test on October 22, according to documents the company filed with the Federal Communications Commission,  CNBC reports. This flight will likely be the first of two that the space tourism company has planned to complete testing of its SpaceShipTwo spacecraft system, and it should have just two test pilots on board.
 Commercial service, finally, next year? ... Virgin Galactic said last month that the second test spaceflight, for which no date has yet been set, will have four "mission specialists" inside the cabin. If both test flights succeed, Virgin Galactic expects to fly founder Sir Richard Branson in the first quarter of 2021. This milestone flight would mark the beginning of the company's commercial tourism service. (submitted by Ken the Bin, JohnCarter17, and DanNeely)
 The easiest way to keep up with Eric Berger's space reporting is to sign up for his newsletter, we'll collect his stories in your inbox.
 LandSpace raises $175 million. Chinese launch firm LandSpace has raised $175 million in series C+ round funding for development of its Zhuque-2 series of methane/liquid-oxygen launch vehicles,  SpaceNews reports. The funding comes two weeks after Chinese competitor launch firm iSpace secured $173 million in series B funding. Both are impressive cash hauls that signal serious intent.
 A powerful small rocket ... LandSpace is working toward an inaugural launch of the Zhuque-2 in June 2021. The 49.5-meter-tall, two-stage Zhuque-2 will be capable of delivering 4,000kg to a 200km low-Earth orbit or 2,000 kilograms to 500kg Sun-synchronous orbit, according to LandSpace. (submitted by platykurtic and Ken the Bin)
 NASA sounding rocket launches DUST-2 mission. A two-stage Black Brant IX suborbital sounding rocket launched on Tuesday from White Sands Missile Range in New Mexico carrying the DUST-2 science mission.  NASA said the rocket launched the payload to an apogee of approximately 346km before descending back to Earth by parachute.
 Cheap access to microgravity ... DUST-2's goal is to study how individual atoms, shed by dying stars and supernovae, stick together. When they do, the atoms form dust grains—some of the basic building blocks of our universe. "What we're trying to do is duplicate what happens in at least two astrophysical environments," said principal investigator Joe Nuth, a planetary scientist at NASA's Goddard Space Flight Center. (submitted by JohnCarter17)
 Blue Origin vets start several new companies. Blue Origin turned 20 years old this week, and although the privately held company hasn't yet put people into space or put a rocket into orbit, it has spawned a new generation of space startups,  Cosmic Log reports. Relativity Space is fairly well known, but more companies are currently emerging from semi-stealth mode.
 Launch and in-space propulsion ... One of them, Stoke Space Technologies, appears to be working on technology to enable the reuse of upper stages. Reach Space Technologies seems to be a propulsion company. And Starfish Space says it is working on an "on-demand, in-space transportation service." We'll add all of them to our list of companies to track! (submitted by Ken the Bin and BH)
 Brazil launch site evaluating proposals. The Brazilian Space Agency says it has begun evaluating 11 proposals from companies interested in launching rockets from the Alcântara Space Center, located on the country's northern Atlantic coast. The site, at just 2 degrees south of the equator, offers prime territory from which to launch equatorial missions.
 Turning to commercial use ... Following an initial analysis, the companies will have until October 30, to finalize their proposals,  Parabolic Arc reports. It is not clear how many companies the Brazilian spaceport will accept, but it seems likely to prioritize companies with mature, or nearly mature, rocket designs. The spaceport has been used, until now, primarily for military launches. (submitted by Ken the Bin)
 Australian rocket firm signs Aussie payload. Gilmour Space Technologies  says it has signed an Australian customer for the first launch of its Eris rocket, no earlier than 2022. Space Machines Company has contracted to launch a 35kg spacecraft to orbit. "This could well be the first Australian payload to be launched to orbit on an Australian rocket, from an Australian launch site," said Adam Gilmour, co-founder and CEO of Gilmour Space.
 Talking a big game by the middle of the decade ... The first Eris rockets are advertised as having the capacity to launch payloads up to 305kg into low-Earth orbit and 215kg into 500km Sun-synchronous orbits. The company says its goals are to reach a flight rate of 12 launches a year by 2025 and to help spur a broadening of the Aussie space industry. (submitted by Ken the Bin and platykurtic)
 Germany considers North Sea launch facility. The German government is studying a proposal from German industry to create a mobile launch pad for satellites in the North Sea. Under the plan, small satellites weighing up to one tonne would be launched with German-built rockets. The Federation of German Industries is lobbying for the project,  the BBC reports.
 A good spot for polar launches ... The pad would be a public-private partnership. German media quote the BDI proposal as saying, "a German launchpad is technically feasible and makes strategic and economic sense." Among the companies that could potentially launch from the platform is Isar Aerospace. (submitted by cpushack, Ken the Bin, and JohnCarter17)
  China launches a secretive spaceplane. Following months of low-key preparations at the Jiuquan Satellite Launch Center, China launched an experimental reusable spacecraft on Friday,  SpaceNews reports. A Long March 2F launch vehicle delivered the spacecraft into orbit following launch at an unspecified time.
 Maybe an X-37B clone? ... "After a period of in-orbit operation, the spacecraft will return to the scheduled landing site in China. It will test reusable technologies during its flight, providing technological support for the peaceful use of space," the Xinhua report stated following the launch. The plane landed after about two days in space.  This Twitter thread by NPR's Geoff Brumfiel provides more interesting details about where the mission landed. (submitted by Ken the Bin)
 Chandrayaan-3 Moon mission may launch next year. India's "Chandrayaan-3" lunar mission is likely to be launched in the first quarter of 2021,  DNA India reports. After the Chandrayaan-2 mission was lost last September, the Indian space agency said it would try again to make a soft landing on the Moon's surface.
 Enter Chandrayaan-3 ... Because the orbiter launched with the Chandrayaan-2 mission remains functional, this follow-up launch will only carry a lander. It is expected to launch on a GSLV Mark III rocket from India's Satish Dhawan Space Center, and India will attempt to become just the fourth country to make a soft landing on the Moon. (submitted by JohnCarter17)
 China still has a toxic rocket problem. On Monday, a Long March 4B rocket launched from China's Taiyuan Satellite Launch Center carrying a remote-sensing satellite. This 50-year-old spaceport is located in north-central China, about 500km to the southwest of Beijing. As often happens with the first stages of Chinese rockets launching from the inland Taiyuan facility, the spent Long March 4B booster fell downstream of the spaceport. In this case, it landed near a school and created a predictably large cloud of toxic gas.
 Living the hydrazine dream (or nightmare) ... The use of hydrazine as a fuel for launch vehicles has been phased out for most of the world,  Ars reports. The last major US rocket to use hydrazine was United Launch Alliance's Delta II rocket, which used the toxic fuel in its second stage. Yet the majority of China's launch fleet is powered by hydrazine fuel and nitrogen tetroxide oxidizer. This includes its human-rated Long March 2F rocket as well as the widely used Long March 4 family.
  Delta IV Heavy launch reset for September 18. United Launch Alliance teams have determined the cause behind a Delta IV Heavy rocket's dramatic, last-second abort late last month,  Florida Today reports. This sets the stage for another attempt no earlier than a week from Friday, and an exact time has not been released. This will be the third attempt to launch the NROL-44 classified mission.
 Rip in the regulator ... On August 29, a torn diaphragm in one of three pressure regulators at Cape Canaveral Air Force Station's Launch Complex 37 caused the computer-controlled scrub just three seconds before liftoff, ULA CEO Tory Bruno  said via Twitter on Wednesday. The engines briefly lit on fire, but the rocket remained firmly on the pad. (submitted by BH and JohnCarter17)
 Military takes wait-and-see approach to super-heavy lift. The US military is happy with the current offerings from United Launch Alliance and SpaceX, but it is keeping an eye on future super-heavy rockets under development. That's according to comments made this week by Brig. Gen. D. Jason Cothern, who oversees launch services procurement for the US Space Force ( SpaceNews reported on his comments).
 If you build it, will they come? ... "We believe the current providers address the plans we have today for the near future." That's what Gen. Cothern said in response to a viewer's question on the potential military value of super-heavy-lift vehicles like Starship and New Glenn, which are being developed to fly to the Moon and beyond. As to what might be required in the next generation of launch vehicles, "as the lead acquirers for military space, it's a question that's dear to us," Cothern said. (submitted by JohnCarter17 and platykurtic)
 SpaceX may roll a test tank to pad this week. The next tests at SpaceX's Boca Chica facility are likely to involve a test tank rather than a full-scale Starship prototype,  NASASpaceflight.com reports. This "SN7.1" model is a larger test tank than its predecessor, SN 7, and made from 304L-series stainless steel (or at least a variant of that alloy). It is likely to be pushed to the bursting point so that SpaceX engineers can understand its limits.
 Prepping for another pop ... While 304L may not be the "final" alloy SpaceX is hoping to utilize on Starships and Super Heavies in the longer-term future, all previous Starships have been made from the 301-series alloy. Future versions, beginning with SN8, will use the new alloy. With two launch mounts available, it remains to be seen if SN7.1 will take up residence on Starship's regular mount or the second test mount. (submitted by platykurtic)
  Sept. 11: Rocket 3.1 | Astra demonstration mission | Kodiak Spaceport, Alaska | 02:00 UTC

note:
微软(Microsoft)警告称，俄罗斯国家黑客组织花式熊(Fight Bear)正瞄准参与美国和英国选举的组织。该组织曾对民主党全国委员会(Democratic National Committee)和世界反兴奋剂机构(World Anti-Dopping Agency)发起粉碎和泄漏攻击，在全球范围内造成数十亿美元损失的NotPetya蠕虫病毒，以及50万台路由器的VPN过滤器漏洞。
微软表示，在上个月的两周时间里，该组织试图攻击28个组织的6900多个账户。在2019年9月至去年6月期间，Fancy Bear瞄准了属于200多个组织员工的数万个账户。黑客使用两种技术--一种被称为“暴力逼迫”，另一种被称为“密码散布”--试图获取攻击目标Office365的登录凭据。到目前为止，没有一次袭击成功。
来自多家公司的安全研究人员普遍认为，Fancy Bear代表俄罗斯GRU军事情报机构工作。GRU与十多年的高级黑客活动有关，其中包括几次对国家安全造成严重损害的黑客活动。行业成员使用各种五颜六色的名称来指代该集团。除了奇幻熊，还有典当风暴，索菲斯，塞德尼特和沙皇团队。微软对这套装备的名字是锶。
微软公司副总裁汤姆·伯特(Tom Burt)在周四发布的一篇帖子中写道，微软威胁情报中心(MSTIC)观察到，从2019年9月到今天，Strontium进行了一系列攻击。与我们在2016年观察到的情况类似，锶正在发起活动，以获取人们的登录凭据或泄露他们的账户，想必是为了帮助情报收集或破坏行动。
锶是微软表示瞄准2020年选举的三个国家支持的黑客组织之一。据信为中华民国服务的锆公司一直把矛头对准与大选有关的知名人士，包括与乔·拜登竞选总统有关的人和国际事务领域的知名领导人。研究人员称，代表伊朗伊斯兰共和国工作的磷公司继续瞄准与唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统连任竞选活动有关联的人的个人账户。“磷”公司一直在瞄准与唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)总统竞选连任有关联的人的个人账户，研究人员表示，该公司代表伊朗伊斯兰共和国工作，一直将目标对准与美国总统唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)的连任竞选活动有关的知名人士。
虽然这三个组织的活动都构成了风险，但鉴于该组织先进的技能和技术，以及其厚颜无耻和危险的黑客记录，来自花花熊的活动带来了最大的威胁。微软随附的一篇帖子提供了有关Fancy Bear黑客活动的技术细节，称该组织自2016年以来已经大幅精简和自动化了运营。
四年前，Fancy Bear严重依赖鱼叉式网络钓鱼，也就是发送看起来令人信服的电子邮件，恶搞谷歌或其他知名组织的人员。众所周知，这些电子邮件诱骗了希拉里·克林顿(Hillary Clinton)的总统竞选主席约翰·波德斯塔(John Podesta)，它们向收件人谎报他们的账户已被窃取。然后，鱼叉鱼指示他们登录一个后来被证明是假网站的网站，并更改他们的密码。
现在，Fancy Bear主要依赖于执行密码喷洒和暴力强制的工具。这一变化使得规模化运营变得更容易，而且更具匿名性。这些工具通过大约1100个IP地址池分发，其中大部分属于ToR匿名化服务。在周四的技术帖子中，微软研究人员写道：
此基础设施池随着时间的推移而发展，平均每天约有20个IP从其中添加和删除。锶的工具大约每秒在此IP池中轮换一次身份验证尝试。考虑到这项技术的广度和速度，锶似乎已经修改了它的工具，以使用匿名器服务来混淆其活动、逃避跟踪和避免归属。
在8月19日至9月3日的攻击中，微软观察到平均每天有1,294个IP地址，来自500多个地址块和250个自治系统编号。其中一些网块比其他网块使用得更频繁。网络块的过度使用为研究人员提供了一个机会，让他们找出使用匿名化服务的奇幻熊(Fancy Bear)活动。Microsoft使用此Azure Sentinel查询从三个最广泛使用的地址块识别失败的身份验证尝试，并按尝试登录的用户代理对其进行分组。
密码喷射，它尝试查找有效的用户名-密码组合。通常，在几天或几周的过程中，每小时大约有四次尝试。几乎每一次尝试都来自不同的IP地址。
暴力强迫，在几个小时或几天的过程中，每小时向目标账户发送约300次登录尝试。
考虑到2016年花式熊黑客攻击的后果，你可能会认为大多数高价值目标自那以后都采用了多因素身份验证，这要求登录者提供正确的密码，并证明拥有一台设备，或者出示指纹或其他生物特征。但根据微软的说法，你错了。该公司去年10月公布的数据显示，只有不到10%的大型组织账户使用任何形式的MFA。微软表示，启用多因素身份验证可以挫败大多数凭证获取攻击。
研究人员建议，在监控账户中的登录活动时，在这些失败的身份验证中寻找任何类型的可识别模式，并随时间的推移进行跟踪。密码喷洒是民族国家行为者越来越常用的策略。
note_en:
Fancy Bear—the Russian state hacking group that brought you the smash-and-leak attacks on the  Democratic National Committee and  World Anti-Doping Agency, the  NotPetya worm that inflicted billions of dollars of damage worldwide, and the  VPN Filter compromise of 500,000 routers—is targeting organizations involved in elections taking place in the US and UK, Microsoft has warned.
 Over a two-week period last month, the group attempted attacks on more than 6,900 accounts belonging to 28 organizations, Microsoft said. Between September 2019 and last June, Fancy Bear targeted tens of thousands of accounts belonging to employees of more than 200 organizations. The hackers use two techniques—one known as "brute forcing" and the other called "password spraying"—in an attempt to obtain targets' Office365 login credentials. So far, none of the attacks has succeeded.
 Security researchers from a host of companies widely agree that Fancy Bear works on behalf of the GRU, Russia's military intelligence agency. The GRU has been tied to more than a decade of advanced hacking campaigns, including several that have inflicted serious damage to national security. Industry members use an assortment of colorful names to refer to the group. Besides Fancy Bear, there's also Pawn Storm, Sofacy, Sednit, and Tsar Team. Microsoft's name for the outfit is Strontium.
 "Microsoft's Threat Intelligence Center (MSTIC) has observed a series of attacks conducted by Strontium between September 2019 and today," Microsoft Corporate Vice President Tom Burt wrote in a  post published on Thursday. "Similar to what we observed in 2016, Strontium is launching campaigns to harvest people's log-in credentials or compromise their accounts, presumably to aid in intelligence gathering or disruption operations."
 Strontium is one of three state-sponsored hacking groups that Microsoft said are targeting the 2020 elections. Zirconium—believed to work for the People's Republic of China—has been targeting "high-profile individuals associated with the election, including people associated with the Joe Biden for President campaign and prominent leaders in the international affairs community." Phosphorus, which researchers say works on behalf of the Islamic Republic of Iran, continues to target personal accounts of people associated with President Donald Trump's reelection campaign.
  While campaigns from all three groups pose a risk, the one from Fancy Bear carries the biggest threat, given the group's advanced skill and techniques and its track record of brazen and dangerous hacks. An  accompanying Microsoft post that provided technical details about the Fancy Bear hacking campaign said the group has streamlined and automated its operations significantly since 2016.
 Four years ago, Fancy Bear leaned heavily on spear phishing, or the sending of convincing-looking emails that spoofed personnel from Google or other well-known organizations. The emails, one that famously  hooked Hillary Clinton's presidential campaign chairman, John Podesta, falsely reported to receivers that their accounts had been compromised. The spearphishes then instructed them to log in to what turned out to be a fake site and change their passwords.
 Now, Fancy Bear is relying primarily on tools that perform password spraying and brute forcing. The change makes it easier to operate at scale and in a way that's more anonymized. The tools are distributed through a pool of roughly 1,100 IP addresses, with most of them belonging to the Tor anonymization service. In Thursday's technical post, Microsoft researchers wrote:
 This pool of infrastructure has evolved over time, with an average of approximately 20 IPs added and removed from it per day. STRONTIUM's tooling alternates its authentication attempts amongst this pool of IPs approximately once per second. Considering the breadth and speed of this technique, it seems likely that STRONTIUM has adapted its tooling to use an anonymizer service to obfuscate its activity, evade tracking, and avoid attribution.
  In the attacks between August 19 and September 3, Microsoft observed a daily average of 1,294 IP addresses from more than 500 address blocks and 250  autonomous system numbers. Some of the netblocks were used more often than others. The overutilization of the netblocks created an opportunity for researchers to ferret out Fancy Bear activity that used the anonymization service. Microsoft used  this Azure Sentinel query to identify failed authentication attempts from the three most widely used address blocks and group them by the user agents attempting to log in.
  Password spraying, which attempts to find valid username-password combinations. Typically, there are about four tries each hour over the course of days or weeks. Almost every attempt originates from a different IP address.
 Brute-forcing, which peppers a targeted account with about 300 login attempts per hour over the course of several hours or days.
  Given the fallout from Fancy Bear's 2016 hacks, you might think that most high-value targets had since adopted multifactor authentication, which requires the person logging in to provide the correct password and to also prove possession of a device or present a fingerprint or other biometric. But according to Microsoft, you'd be wrong.  Figures the company published last October show that less than 10 percent of large-organization accounts use any form of MFA. Turning multifactor authentication on thwarts most credential-harvesting attacks, Microsoft said.
  "When monitoring login activity in your accounts, look for any type of discernible patterns in these failed authentications and track them over time," researchers advised.  Password spray is an increasingly common tactic of nation-state actors."

note:
查理·博尔登(Charlie Bolden)曾四次担任宇航员，从2009年年中到2017年初担任NASA局长。在此期间，他监督了该机构大型太空发射系统火箭的创建和初步开发。
尽管美国国家航空航天局(NASA)一些官员，如时任副局长洛里·加弗(Lori Garver)，对火箭的成本持谨慎态度-目前已投入约200亿美元用于基于现有技术的运载火箭开发-博尔登仍然是这枚大型火箭的捍卫者，称其是该机构将人类送出近地轨道(可能是月球或火星)计划的关键。他还对SpaceX等商业太空公司建造类似技术的努力不屑一顾。
2014年，当我在约翰逊航天中心(Johnson Space Center)与博尔登坐下来接受采访时，我问道，当SpaceX使用自有资金开发成本较低的猎鹰重型火箭时，为什么NASA要在SLS火箭上投入这么多资金。他当时的回应是：“让我们非常诚实。我们没有商用的重型运输车。猎鹰9号重型猎鹰有朝一日可能会问世。它现在就在绘图板上。SLS是真实存在的。“。
两年后的2016年，博尔登表示，他仍然不相信商业公司能胜任这项任务。如果你谈到运载火箭，我们相信我们对国家的责任是照顾那些普通人做不到或不想做的事情，比如大型运载火箭，博尔登说。我现在还不是大型运载火箭商业投资的狂热粉丝。
从那时起，很多事情都发生了变化。2018年2月，SpaceX首次发射猎鹰重型火箭。自那以后，它又成功飞行了两次，它将在美国国家航空航天局(NASA)未来的探索计划中发挥作用。与此同时，原定于2017年发射的SLS火箭现在被推迟到至少2021年底。
因此，博尔登似乎改变了主意。在周五早上出版的“太空通讯”上刊登的Politico采访中，博尔登被问及未来四年可能会发生什么。
“SLS会消失的，”他说。它可能会在拜登政府或下一届特朗普政府的…期间消失。因为在某一时刻，商业实体将迎头赶上。他们真的要建造一种类似SLS的重型运载火箭，这样他们就能以比NASA做SLS便宜得多的价格飞行。这就是它的运作方式。“。
博尔登在太空界仍然是一个受欢迎和有影响力的声音，但他对美国的太空政策不再有直接发言权。也许因为他不再需要就NASA的预算向国会负责，他也可以自由地说出自己的想法。无论如何，他的言论反映了航天界的普遍情绪-至少在波音(Boeing)和诺斯罗普·格鲁曼(Northrop Grumman)等直接受益于SLS开发的传统承包商之外-SLS火箭最终会消失。
猎鹰重型火箭没有SLS火箭那么有能力，但它的成功清楚地表明，私营公司可以制造大型、强大的火箭。此外，寻求用私人资金建造重型升降式火箭的不仅仅是SpaceX，还有拥有New Glenn助推器的Blue Origin。尽管是竞争对手，SpaceX的埃隆·马斯克和Blue Origin的杰夫·贝佐斯都认为火箭需要能够重复使用才能生存。SLS的发射成本约为20亿美元，然后落入大海。
如果你想知道商业航天支持者对SLS火箭的真实想法，因为它的成本和可消耗性，它来自一家新的航天公司的一位高级官员：
如果圣诞老人来了，他说，我有个好消息。它现在可以工作了，你可以明天发射。一切都做好了。你们明天将有一个发布会。&#39；。它仍然不能带我们去月球。即使他们实现了所有的目标，它仍然不能把人们送上月球。它当然不会在月球上建立基地，也绝对不会把人类送上火星。
当国会在2010年构思太空发射系统火箭并指示NASA建造它时，他们下了两个赌注。首先，他们押注SpaceX等新的太空公司将会失败。这在当时是一个合理的押注，因为SpaceX公司已经失去了他们试图发射到太空的大部分火箭。其次，他们打赌像波音这样的传统公司在制造大型火箭方面会做得更好。
创建SLS的国会议员-最初是佛罗里达州参议员比尔·纳尔逊(Bill Nelson)和德克萨斯州参议员凯·贝利·哈奇森(Kay Bailey Hutchison)，很快阿拉巴马州参议员理查德·谢尔比(Richard Shelby)也加入了他们的行列-这两次押注都失败了。所以现在美国国家航空航天局(NASA)正在建造大型的消耗性火箭，这已经花费了纳税人数百亿美元。国会仍然一如既往地致力于预算和公开支持声明。然而，新火箭飞得越多，这种支持就越难维持。
具有讽刺意味的是，NASA和SLS主承包商波音公司不再与猎鹰重型飞机竞争。SpaceX在2.5年前击败了他们。相反，美国国家航空航天局(NASA)正在与SpaceX的下一款火箭--将把“星际飞船”送入轨道的超重型助推器--展开竞争。SpaceX甚至还没有建造其超重型火箭的一个部件--它比SLS更大，更强大，价格低得多，而且可重复使用--但它有可能在2021年10年前的SLS之前进行轨道发射。
note_en:
Charlie Bolden, a four-time astronaut, served as NASA Administrator from mid-2009 through early 2017. During that time, he oversaw the creation and initial development of the agency's large Space Launch System rocket.
 Although some NASA officials such as then-deputy director Lori Garver were wary of the rocket's costs— about $20 billion has now been poured into development of a launch vehicle based on existing technology—Bolden remained a defender of the large rocket, calling it a lynchpin of the agency's plans to send humans beyond low-Earth orbit, perhaps to the Moon or Mars. He also dismissed the efforts of commercial space companies like SpaceX to build comparable technology.
 When I sat down with Bolden for an interview in 2014 at Johnson Space Center, I asked why NASA was investing so much in the SLS rocket when SpaceX was using its own funds to develop the lower-cost Falcon Heavy rocket. His  response at the time: “Let’s be very honest. We don’t have a commercially available heavy-lift vehicle. The Falcon 9 Heavy may some day come about. It’s on the drawing board right now. SLS is real.”
  Two years later, in 2016,  Bolden said he still did not believe commercial companies were up to the task. "If you talk about launch vehicles, we believe our responsibility to the nation is to take care of things that normal people cannot do, or don’t want to do, like large launch vehicles," Bolden said. "I’m not a big fan of commercial investment in large launch vehicles just yet."
 Since that time, a lot has changed. In February, 2018, SpaceX  launched the Falcon Heavy rocket for the first time. It has since flown successfully two more times, and it will play a role in NASA's future exploration plans. Meanwhile, the SLS rocket, originally due to launch in 2017, is now delayed until at least the end of 2021.
 As a result of this, Bolden appears to have changed his mind. In an interview with  Politico  published Friday morning in the publication's Space newsletter, Bolden was asked what might happen during the next four years.
 “SLS will go away," he said. "It could go away during a Biden administration or a next Trump administration… because at some point commercial entities are going to catch up. They are really going to build a heavy lift launch vehicle sort of like SLS that they will be able to fly for a much cheaper price than NASA can do SLS. That’s just the way it works.”
 Bolden remains a popular and influential voice in the space community, but he no longer has a direct say in US space policy. Perhaps because he no longer has to answer to Congress for NASA budgets, he is also free to speak his mind. In any case, his comments reflect the general sentiment in the space community—at least outside of the traditional contractors like Boeing and Northrop Grumman who directly benefit from SLS development—that the SLS rocket will eventually go away.
  The Falcon Heavy is not as capable as the SLS rocket, but its success has clearly demonstrated that private companies can build large, powerful rockets. Moreover it is not just SpaceX, but Blue Origin with its New Glenn booster, that seek to build heavy lift rockets with private money. And although they are rivals, SpaceX's Elon Musk and Blue Origin's Jeff Bezos both agree that rockets need to be capable of reuse to be viable. The SLS will cost about $2 billion to launch, and then fall into the ocean.
 If you're wondering what commercial space proponents really think about the SLS rocket due to its cost and expendability, it's this, which comes from a senior official at a new space company:
  "If Santa Claus arrived, and said, 'I have good news. It now works and you can launch tomorrow. Everything's done. You're going to have a launch tomorrow.' ... It still isn't getting us to the Moon. Even if they achieve everything they aim for, it still does not get people to the Moon. It certainly does not get a base on the Moon and absolutely doesn't get humans to Mars."
 When Congress conceived of the Space Launch System rocket in 2010 and directed NASA to build it, they were making two bets. First, they bet the new space companies such as SpaceX would fail. This was a reasonable bet back then, as SpaceX had lost most of the rockets they'd tried to launch into space. Second, they bet that traditional companies like Boeing would be better at building big rockets.
  The Congressional lawmakers who created SLS—it began with Florida Senator Bill Nelson and Texas Senator Kay Bailey Hutchison, and they were soon joined by Alabama Senator Richard Shelby—lost both of those bets. And so now NASA is building large, expendable rocket that has cost taxpayers 10s of billions of dollars. Congress remains as committed as ever, both in budgets and public statements of support. However, the more that new rockets fly, the more difficult this support will be to maintain.
 Ironically, NASA and the SLS prime contractor Boeing are no longer competing  with the Falcon Heavy. SpaceX beat them 2.5 years ago. Rather, NASA is competing with SpaceX's  next rocket, the Super Heavy booster that will loft Starship into orbit. SpaceX has not even built a single segment of  its Super Heavy rocket—which is larger than SLS, more powerful, vastly cheaper, and resuable—but it is possible that vehicle makes an orbital launch before the decade-old SLS in 2021.

note:
当微软支持两周前在Epic Games对苹果的反垄断诉讼中提出的一项关键动议时，令一些人感到惊讶。
20年前，美国司法部、18个州和哥伦比亚特区起诉微软，指控微软利用Windows操作系统垄断当时羽翼未丰的IE浏览器，违反了1890年的谢尔曼反垄断法(Sherman Anti Trust Act)。诉讼明确声称，微软使用Windows冻结了Netscape浏览器，更心照不宣地冻结了Sun Microsystems的跨平台Java平台。
这家软件制造商对这些指控怒不可遏，并声称这一行动代表着政府应无法在价值上竞争的公司的要求提出的入侵。微软警告称，这一行动将开创一个危险的先例，可能会在未来几年扼杀创新。
该公司的法律回应是我做记者25年来报道过的最有力的回应之一。公关活动也达到了令人震惊和敬畏的程度，操纵者一度安排了一场执行不力的天文地盘运动，目的是激发公众舆论反对这一法律行动。
现在，在2020年，微软的一名高管提交了支持原告Epic Games的宣誓书面证词，声称苹果“完全垄断了向ios…的10亿用户分发应用程序。以迫使应用程序开发者使用苹果的支付平台。“。
微软游戏开发商Experience总经理凯文·甘米尔发表了这份法律声明，以回应苹果公司的威胁，苹果公司威胁称，Epicc不能使用其在iOS上开发虚幻引擎游戏平台所需的软件开发工具。在史诗娱乐试图在iOS版的“堡垒之夜”中使用自己的支付系统来规避苹果30%的平台费后，苹果发出了这一威胁。这一举动很快使这款游戏从苹果应用商店下架，并导致Etic提起诉讼作为回应。
甘米尔表示，任何损害EpiceUnreal Engine在iOS上开发的举动都会损害微软的业务，因为在微软看来，创建者几乎没有其他选择可以让创建者在包括iOS在内的多个平台上获得与Unreal Engine一样多的特性和功能的许可。
尽管微软提交的文件没有对潜在的反垄断指控发表意见，但这份声明表明，一度对反垄断法律和理论不屑一顾的公司文化出现了戏剧性的转变。微软不再是曾经的巨人了-很大程度上是因为谷歌(Google)、Facebook和苹果(Apple)等公司的崛起，微软签署了一项和解协议-微软现在可以轻松地支持科技行业的大卫们。
8月份提交的文件也代表着另一种重大逆转。在20世纪90年代中后期，当导致反垄断诉讼的事件正在上演时，苹果正在维持生命。微软知道这一点，根据政府的诉讼，该公司利用Mac制造商作为棋子，以削弱Navigator和Java对Windows垄断构成的威胁。现在，苹果代表了微软帮助杀死的巨人。
所有这一切让我想起了我对微软反垄断审判报道中最令人难忘的部分之一-公司联合创始人、当时的首席执行官兼董事长比尔·盖茨(Bill Gates)的视频证词。在最基本的层面上，这份证词突显了他对一项行为的彻底蔑视，他认为这一行为侵犯了他的公司-以及其他公司-按照自己认为合适的方式设计产品和开展业务的能力。
在为期三天的证词陈述中，策略是典型的微软。阻碍。把政府描绘成脱离实际的政策书呆子，不知道科技和真实市场是如何运作的。最重要的是，在政府的情况下，甚至否认最基本的前提。盖茨的律师和公关人员大军的计划似乎是利用他作为软件神童的形象，他从哈佛辍学，创办了自己的公司，后来成为世界上最富有的人。盖茨团队计划用同样专横的意志力击退政府律师。
到了第二天，情况变得很明显，战略正在壮观地失败。正如“纽约客”作家肯·奥莱塔(Ken Auletta)曾经指出的那样，盖茨一生中从未对工作卑躬屈膝，也从未遭受过我们大多数人经常经历的许多屈辱。他经常斥责记者问一些他会说是愚蠢的问题。盖茨被公开称赞为睿智的圣人、完美的商人和行业远见卓识的人，他习惯了受到除少数同行之外的所有同行的奉承。因此，他几乎没有或根本没有容忍-更不用说遇到-异见、批评或对他权威的挑战的经验。
缺乏经验正好对政府有利。法庭视频没有描绘一个控制自己领域的领导人，对他的案件和他的公司的法律和道德正义充满信心，而是展示了盖茨以前从未公开展示的一面。他脾气暴躁，心胸狭隘，心慌意乱，闷闷不乐。他是个无能为力的人。一言以蔽之，他被打了。
在为期三天的紧张质询中，盖茨经常假装对自己公司的政策和行动一无所知。他分析日常词汇或短语，如“关心”、“支持”和“继续小便”。盖茨似乎利用这一战略来回避尖锐的问题，即他的公司是否滥用其根深蒂固的Windows特许经营权，扼杀了新兴的竞争对手，如Navigator和Java。令他和他的许多律师和形象处理员感到惊讶的是，盖茨给人的印象是好辩、小气，是一个在更强大的竞争对手面前严重失利的人。
一个例子是在与司法部聘请的私人律师大卫·博伊斯(David Boies)的交流中：
男孩：关于“关心”这个词，你有什么不明白的吗？
男孩：“关心”这个词在英语里是不是你很熟悉的一个术语？
note_en:
When Microsoft backed a key motion filed two weeks ago in  Epic Games’ antitrust lawsuit against Apple, it raised a few eyebrows.
 Two decades ago, the US Justice Department, 18 states, and the District of Columbia sued Microsoft on allegations the Windows operating system represented a monopoly that the company was wielding to prop up its then fledgling Internet Explorer browser, in violation of the  Sherman Antitrust Act of 1890. The suit expressly claimed that Microsoft was using Windows to freeze out the Netscape browser and, more tacitly, Sun Microsystems’ cross-platform Java platform as well.
 The software maker vehemently bristled at the allegations and claimed that the action represented a government intrusion brought at the behest of companies that couldn’t compete on the merits. Microsoft warned that the action would set a dangerous precedent that could stifle innovation for years to come.
 The company's legal response was one of the most vigorous I’ve covered in my 25 years as a journalist. The PR campaign reached shock-and-awe proportions, as well, with handlers at one point arranging a  poorly executed astro turf campaign intended to galvanize public opinion against the legal action.
 Now, in 2020, a Microsoft executive has submitted sworn written testimony in support of plaintiff Epic Games alleging that Apple has a “ complete monopoly over the distribution of apps to the billion users of iOS … to coerce app developers into using Apple’s payment platform.”
 The  legal declaration, from Microsoft Gaming Developer Experiences General Manager Kevin Gammill, came in response to Apple's threat to deny Epic access to software development tools it needed to develop its Unreal Engine game platform for use on iOS. Apple made the threat after Epic tried to use its own payment system in the iOS version of  Fortnite to get around Apple's 30-percent platform fee. That move quickly got the game pulled from the Apple App Store and led Epic to file a lawsuit in response.
 Gammill said that any move harming development of Epic's Unreal Engine on iOS would hurt Microsoft's business, because "in Microsoft’s view there are very few other options available for creators to license with as many features and as much functionality as Unreal Engine across multiple platforms, including iOS."
  While Microsoft’s filing expressed no opinion on the underlying antitrust claims, the declaration nonetheless illustrated a dramatic about-face for a company culture that once wore its contempt for antitrust law and theory on its sleeve. No longer the Goliath it once was—in large part because of the ascendance of companies like Google, Facebook, and Apple made possible by a settlement Microsoft signed—Microsoft was now comfortable supporting the Davids of the tech industry.
 The August filing also represented a major reversal of another sort. In the mid to late 1990s when the events leading to the antitrust suit were playing out, Apple was on life support. Microsoft knew this, and according to the government suit, the company used the maker of Macs as a pawn in a bid to blunt the threat Navigator and Java posed to the Windows monopoly. Now, Apple represents the Goliath Microsoft was helping to slay.
 All of this got me thinking about the one of the most memorable parts of my coverage of Microsoft antitrust trial—the videotaped deposition of Bill Gates, the company’s cofounder and at the time its CEO and chairman. At its most basic level, the deposition underscored the utter contempt he had for an action he believed impinged the ability of his company—and others to follow, he warned—to design products and conduct business as they saw fit.
 The strategy during the three-day deposition was classic Microsoft. Obstruct. Paint the government as out-of-touch policy wonks who had no idea how tech and real markets worked. And above all, deny even the most basic of premises in the government’s case. The plan from Gates’ army of lawyers and PR handlers seemed to be to wield his image as a software wunderkind who dropped out of Harvard to bootstrap his company and went on to become the world’s richest man. Team Gates planned to use that same domineering force of will to beat back government lawyers.
  By day 2, it became clear that strategy was failing spectacularly. As New Yorker writer Ken Auletta once noted, Gates had never in his life groveled for a job or suffered many of the indignities most of us experience on a regular basis. He regularly berated reporters for asking what he’d say were stupid questions. Publicly lauded as the wise sage, consummate businessman, and industry visionary, Gates was accustomed to being treated with obsequious deference from all but a small number of peers. As such, he had little or no experience tolerating—let alone encountering—dissent, criticism, or challenges to his authority.
 The lack of experience played right into the government’s hand. Instead of portraying a leader in control of his domain and confident in his case and his company’s legal and ethical righteousness, the courtroom videos showed a side of Gates that had never been on public display before. He was petulant, petty, flustered, and dour. He was ineffectual. He was, in a word, beaten.
 During three days of intense questioning, Gates often feigned ignorance of his own company’s policies and actions. He parsed everyday words or phrases such as “concern,” “support,” and “piss on.” Gates seemed to use the strategy to evade tough questions about whether his company abused its entrenched Windows franchise to kill off emerging competitors, such as Navigator and Java. To the surprise of him and his many attorneys and image handlers, Gates came off as argumentative, petty, and someone badly losing ground to a more formidable rival.
 One example came in this exchange with David Boies, the private attorney hired by the Justice Department:
 Boies: What is it about the word “concerned” that you don’t understand?
    Boies: Is the term “concerned” a term that you’re familiar with in the English language?
title: 重温比尔·盖茨证词这一壮观的失败
title_en: Revisiting the spectacular failure that was the Bill Gates deposition

在家工作和上学是新常态的一部分。建立一个舒适、舒适、高效的家庭办公环境可能会很困难，也很耗时，但对你来说幸运的是，我们在Ars已经在家工作多年了。
当然，作为有洞察力的技术鉴赏家，我们有一些精心挑选的小工具和家具，让您的家庭办公室设置舒适而高效。如果您已经掌握了基本知识，我们还为您的工作空间提供了一些不错的升级。
UltraSharp 49是戴尔的大男孩显示器，UltraSharp 34是它同样宽宏大量的弟弟。它们的尺寸分别为宽49英寸和34英寸，对角线上的尺寸分别为49英寸和34英寸，但并不傲慢。49的最大分辨率为5,120 x 1,440，刷新率为60 Hz，而34的最大分辨率为3440 x 1440，刷新率也为60 Hz。
正如你可能已经注意到的，49英寸的宽高比为32：9，这使得它既宽又窄，基本上形成了两个27英寸的QHD(2560x1440)显示器无缝连接成一个。事实上，除了将这款超宽屏用作一台电脑的单一、无缝“双”显示器外，你还可以使用戴尔的逐图功能将显示器一分为二，并排显示两台独立的电脑。您甚至可以通过戴尔内置的KVM功能在它们之间交换鼠标和键盘，并在Windows计算机上使用热键触发交换。
34英寸的长宽比为21：9，具有相同的功能，但在一块49英寸的屏幕上使用两台电脑明显比34英寸要少得多，尽管这一功能在任何一台显示器上都很有用。这两款显示器都有两个HDMI端口、一个DisplayPort、至少四个USB 3.0下游端口(49英寸有五个)、两个USB 3.0上行端口和带供电功能的USB-C，为您的设备充电。
(Ars Technica可能会通过会员计划从本帖子上的链接获得销售补偿。)。
就画质而言，您不会因工作需要而有所欠缺。颜色看起来非常准确，细节也很清晰。您还可以根据白天(或晚上)的不同时间或不同的应用程序调整此显示器的颜色。这种灵活性以及简单、直观的菜单和快捷选项使UltraSharp 49和34在漫长的工作日中都可以轻松使用。
他们也可以玩游戏，虽然60赫兹是所有分辨率的刷新率上限，这可能不会降低核心玩家的刷新率。
如果你是MacBook的用户，似乎你必须使用非英特尔独立GPU的笔记本，才能将49的分辨率正确配置到5120x1440的最大分辨率。否则，您将停留在3840x1080，这会稍微模糊清晰度，尽管它仍然完全可用并且很容易习惯。我测试的HP Spectre Elite x360和Dell XPS 13都没有这些问题，显示出完美清晰的全分辨率图像，没有任何大惊小怪的地方。
此外，在Windows PC上，您还可以使用戴尔的软件来调整某些内容，比如为KVM设置热键，并确保所有打开的窗口的排列方式与每次插入和拔出计算机时完全相同。虽然前者的功能是Windows独有的，但在没有戴尔Display Manager软件的Mac上，显示器似乎能很好地记住窗口排列。
尽管如此，即使我的13英寸MacBook Pro显示的分辨率略有降低，这些显示器极其可用的尺寸仍然是提高工作效率的绝佳助手，而它们的控制和灵活性是如此直观和快速，以至于我很难想象回到更小的双显示器设置。
像飞利浦Brilliance 499P9H这样的竞争对手，虽然也很宽敞，看起来也不错，但没有我们非常欣赏的Dells在不同的照明场景中正确调整显示屏的粒度控制。此外，该型号的电缆管理很差，菜单也不直观，令人沮丧。几个月过去了，我仍然没有点击我想要点击的按钮，部分原因是屏幕上没有视觉表示，但是布局对你也没有好处。
如果你想买一台49英寸或34英寸的超宽屏，戴尔的产品不会让你失望的。
U2720QM拥有戴尔49英寸和34英寸曲面显示器的许多我们喜欢的功能，在更小的27英寸平板显示器包装中为您提供了所有这些好处。它不像更大的超宽屏那样具有“逐个画面”模式，即允许两个单独的计算机输入，但在这种大小下，无论如何这都不是最好的体验。
否则，您仍然可以获得类似的直观菜单、清晰的画质和高度可定制的色彩配置文件，以及可通过DisplayPort 1.4、HDMI 2.0或USB-C供电显示的选项。
如果USB-C功能和通过显示器为您的设备充电对您来说并不重要，但PBP模式才是重要的，那么最新的Dell S2721QS是您的次佳选择，尺寸为27英寸，价格便宜约200美元。由于尺寸较小，它们几乎适用于任何桌面区域，并且还可以旋转，因此屏幕可以在随附的支架上水平使用。
(Ars Technica可能会通过会员计划从本帖子上的链接获得销售补偿。)。
USB-C终于成为电子制造商的标准-即使是最近的iPhone也配备了USB-C to Lightning电缆。与其让你所有设备的充电器堵塞你的工作空间，最好是用一体式桌面充电解决方案来省去这种杂乱和线缆纠结。当然，在这样做的过程中，您不会想要损失任何充电性能。
(Ars Technica可能会通过会员计划从本帖子上的链接获得销售补偿。)。
Nekteck的5端口111W USB-C壁式充电器在这里是一个强大的附加产品，可同时提供USB-C和USB-A充电。其87W USB-C PD端口可以最高速度或至少接近最高速度为最新MacBook Pro或Dell XPS 15等耗电笔记本电脑充电，同时还可以以最快的速度为大多数智能手机和平板电脑充电。除了用于同时为多个设备充电的USB-A端口外，还有四个方便的USB-A端口，Nekteck的包装盒中还包括一根快速充电的USB-C电缆。该设备是USB的-如果也通过了认证，所以你可以放心，它不会烧毁你的任何设备。它长超过6英寸，宽约3英寸，不是目前最小的台式充电器，但如果你想要指尖提供重型电源，这款充电器可以提供。
如果你已经为桌面充电器做好了准备，并且想要一个可以在家里移动的更移动的壁式充电器，可以考虑RAVPower的RP-PC128。这包括两个USB-C PD充电端口，当两个设备同时连接时，这些端口可以单独供电高达90W或分开供电。例如，你可以为两台功率较低的笔记本电脑充电，每台45W，或者在你的手机最高充电到18W时，给更强大的设备充电60W。这款充电器不是USB充电器--如果通过认证的话，但它在网络上收到了很高的反馈，并提供了18个月的保修。它配有电缆，并且是氮化镓(GaN)充电器，2.5x2.5x1.2英寸的体积非常紧凑。最重要的是，它的零售价通常不到50美元，这使得它提供的性能和多功能性非常物有所值。
这些都是笼统的推荐，但对于那些想花更少的时间等待设备充电的人来说，市面上还有很多其他值得一试的快速充电器：
Nekteck的72W 4端口USB-C壁式充电器是另一款优秀的壁式充电器-如果您不需要太多电力，它的USB-C PD端口最大功率为60W，如果您通过USB认证的壁式充电器，它的最大功率为60W，这仍然足以全速为大多数移动设备和许多笔记本电脑充电-并且会更喜欢更小、更便宜的东西。
如果您需要USB-A端口，Aukey的PA-B3是一款高质量的双端口65W充电器。这是另一个GaN充电器，这使得它类似于口袋大小。
如果您需要更多的USB-C电缆来利用所有这些功能，我们可以保证用于iOS设备的Anker的电力线II USB-C to Lightning电缆的耐用性和安全性，以及用于笔记本电脑、任天堂交换机和Android设备的Nekteck的USB-C和迅雷3电缆的耐用性和安全性。
note_en:
Working and schooling from home are part of the new normal. Putting together a home office setup that's pleasant, comfortable, and productive can be tough and time consuming, but lucky for you, we’ve been working from home for years here at Ars.
  Naturally, as discerning tech connoisseurs, we have some well-curated picks for all the gadgets and furniture you need to make your home office setup cozy and productive. We also threw in a few nice-to-have upgrades for your workspace if you've already got the basics down.
      The  UltraSharp 49 is Dell’s big boy monitor and the  UltraSharp 34 is its similarly expansive little brother. They measure in at a wide-but-not-overbearing 49 and 34 inches, respectively, measured diagonally. The 49 maxes out at a 5,120 x 1,440 resolution with a 60Hz refresh rate, while the 34 does 3440 x 1440 also at 60Hz.
 As you may have noticed, the 49-inch has a 32:9 aspect ratio that makes it wide and narrow, essentially forming two 27-inch QHD (2560 x 1440) displays joined seamlessly into one. In fact, in addition to using this ultrawide as a single, seamless “dual” monitor for one computer, you can use Dell’s Picture-by-Picture feature to split the monitor in half and display two separate computers side-by-side. You can even swap your mouse and keyboard between them via Dell’s built-in KVM function and use hotkeys to trigger the swap on Windows machines.
 The 34-inch has this same functionality in its 21:9 aspect ratio, but using two computers on one 49-inch screen is significantly less cramped than on a 34, though this function is quite usable on either display. Both monitors have two HDMI ports, one DisplayPort, at least four USB 3.0 downstream ports (the 49-inch has five), two USB 3.0 upstreams, and USB-C with power delivery to charge your device.
 (Ars Technica may earn compensation for sales from links on this post through  affiliate programs.)
As far as picture quality goes, you won’t be left wanting for your work needs. Colors look plenty accurate and details are crisp. You also have a ton of range to tweak this monitor’s colors for different times of day (or night) or various applications. This flexibility and the simple, intuitive menu and shortcut options make the UltraSharp 49 and 34 easy to live in for long workdays.
 They can do for gaming too, though 60Hz is the refresh rate cap in all resolutions, which might not cut it for hardcore players.
 If you’re a MacBook user, it  seems you’ll have to use a notebook with a non-Intel discrete GPU to properly configure the 49’s resolution to its 5,120 x 1,440 max. Otherwise, you’re stuck at 3,840 x 1,080 which fudges the sharpness a bit, though it’s still entirely usable and easy to get used to. Neither the HP Spectre Elite x360 nor the Dell XPS 13 I tested had these issues, displaying perfectly crisp, full-resolution imagery without any fuss.
 Furthermore, with Windows PCs, you can also use Dell’s software to tweak certain things, like setting up hotkeys for the KVM and ensuring that all your open windows arrange just as you had them each time you plug and unplug your computer. While the former function is Windows exclusive, the monitor seemed to remember window arrangements just fine on my Mac without Dell’s Display Manager software.
 Still, even with the slightly diminished resolution displayed from my 13-inch MacBook Pro, the extremely usable size of these monitors is a beautiful aid to productivity, while their controls and flexibility are so intuitive and quick that it’s hard for me to imagine going back to a smaller, dual-monitor setup.
 Competitors like the  Philips Brilliance 499P9H, while also expansive and nice to look at, don’t have the granular controls to adjust this display properly in different lighting scenarios that we so appreciated on the Dells. Furthermore, cable management on this model is poor and the menus are frustratingly unintuitive. It’s been months and I still never click the buttons I intend to, due in part to there being no visual representation on the screen, but the layout does you no favors either.
 If you’re in the market for a 49- or 34-inch ultrawide, Dell’s versions won’t let you down.
          With many of the same features we love from Dell’s 49- and 34-inch curved monitors,  the U2720QM offers you all that goodness in a smaller 27-inch flatscreen package. It doesn’t have the “picture-by-picture” mode which allows two separate computer inputs, as the bigger ultrawides do, but at this size that wouldn’t be the greatest experience anyway.
 Otherwise you still get similarly intuitive menus, crisp picture quality, and highly customizable color profile, as well as options to display through DisplayPort 1.4, HDMI 2.0, or USB-C with power delivery.
 If USB-C capability and charging your device through the monitor aren’t important to you but a PBP mode is, then  the latest Dell S2721QS is your next best choice at 27 inches and about $200 less. Thanks to their smaller size, they suit most any desktop area and can also rotate so the screen can be used horizontally on their included stands.
       (Ars Technica may earn compensation for sales from links on this post through  affiliate programs.)
      USB-C is finally the norm among electronic manufacturers—even recent iPhones ship with a USB-C to Lightning cable. Rather than have a bunch of chargers for all your devices clogging up your workspace, it’s best to save that clutter and cable entanglement with an all-in-one desktop charging solution. Of course, in doing so, you don’t want to lose a drop of charging performance.
 (Ars Technica may earn compensation for sales from links on this post through  affiliate programs.)
 Nekteck’s 5-Port 111W USB-C Wall Charger is a robust addition here that offers both USB-C and USB-A charging. Its 87W USB-C PD port can charge power-hungry laptops like the latest MacBook Pro or Dell XPS 15 at, or at least close to, maximum speeds, while also topping up most smartphones and tablets at their fastest rates. There are four handy USB-A ports alongside that for charging multiple devices simultaneously, and Nekteck includes a fast-charging USB-C cable in the box. The device is  USB-IF certified, too, so you can feel confident that it won’t fry any of your devices. At more than six inches long and about three inches wide, it’s not the smallest desktop charger around, but if you want heavy-duty power at your fingertips, this can supply it.
 If you’re all squared away for desktop chargers and would prefer a more mobile wall charger that you can move around the house, consider  RAVPower’s RP-PC128. This includes two USB-C PD charging ports that can individually supply up to 90W or split that power when two devices are connected simultaneously. You could charge two lower-power laptops at 45W each, for instance, or have a more powerful device charge at 60W while your phone maxes out at 18W. This charger is not USB-IF certified, but it’s received high feedback around the Web and it comes with an 18-month warranty. It comes paired with a cable, and being a  gallium nitride (GaN) charger, it’s nicely compact at 2.5 x 2.5 x 1.2 inches. Best of all, it usually retails for less than $50, making it great value for the performance and versatility it provides.
 These are blanket recommendations, but there are plenty other worthwhile fast chargers out there for those who want to spend less time waiting for their devices to refill:
 Nekteck’s 72W 4-Port USB-C Wall Charger is another excellent and USB-IF certified wall charger if you don’t need quite as much power—its USB-C PD port maxes at 60W, which is still enough to refill most mobile devices and many laptops at full speed—and would prefer something smaller and less expensive.
 Aukey’s PA-B3 is a quality dual-port 65W charger if you need a USB-A port. It's another GaN charger, which makes it similarly pocket-sized.
 And if you need more USB-C cables to take advantage of all this power, we can vouch for the durability and safety of  Anker’s PowerLine II USB-C to Lightning cables for iOS devices, and  Nekteck’s USB-C and  Thunderbolt 3 cables for laptops, the Nintendo Switch, and Android devices.

note:
微软周四表示，2016年入侵民主党人的俄罗斯军事情报机构也曾试图对包括政党和顾问在内的200多个组织的计算机系统进行类似的入侵。
该公司表示，这些努力似乎是针对美国政治竞选和相关团体的更广泛增加的一部分。微软副总裁汤姆·伯特(Tom Burt)在一篇博客文章中表示：“我们看到的情况与之前的攻击模式一致，这些攻击模式不仅针对候选人和竞选工作人员，还针对他们在关键问题上咨询的人。”
他说，俄罗斯、中国和伊朗特工的大多数渗透企图都被微软安全软件阻止，并通知了目标。该公司不愿就谁可能成功遭到黑客攻击或影响发表评论。
微软没有评估哪个外国对手对11月总统选举的诚信构成更大的威胁。网络安全专家的共识是，俄罗斯的干预是最严重的。特朗普政府高级官员对此提出异议，尽管没有提供任何证据。
顶级网络安全公司火眼(FireEye)的情报分析总监约翰·胡尔特奎斯特(John Hultquist)说，“这是2016年的演员，可能会像往常一样行事。”“我们认为，俄罗斯军事情报继续对民主进程构成最大威胁。”
Hultquist说，微软的帖子显示，俄罗斯军事情报部门继续追查与选举相关的目标，没有被美国的起诉、制裁和其他对策吓倒。国会和联邦调查局(FBI)调查人员发现，它通过黑客攻击民主党全国委员会(Democratic National Committee)和希拉里·克林顿(Hillary Clinton)的竞选经理约翰·波德斯塔(John Podesta)的电子邮件，并在网上发布令人尴尬的材料，干预了2016年的竞选活动，试图让特朗普的竞选活动受益。
微软确认是当前选举相关活动幕后黑手的GRU军事情报单位花花熊(Fancy Bear)也在2016年侵入了至少三个州的选民登记数据库，尽管没有证据表明它试图干预投票。
微软之所以能看到这些努力，是因为它的软件无处不在，安全性评级也很高。微软没有提到管理选举或操作投票系统的美国官员今年是否成为了国家支持的黑客的攻击目标。美国情报官员表示，到目前为止，他们还没有看到这方面的证据。他们上个月表示，俄罗斯人支持特朗普总统，中国人更喜欢民主党挑战者、前副总统乔·拜登(Joe Biden)。
在“华盛顿邮报”(Washington Post)本周的一篇评论文章中，曾在2017年至2019年担任国家情报副总监的苏珊·戈登(Susan Gordon)表示，“俄罗斯的意图是破坏美国的民主”，而中国则试图影响美国的政策，削弱美国的全球影响力，这在一定程度上是通过窃取知识产权来实现的。
约翰霍普金斯大学的地缘政治专家托马斯里德说，他对微软拒绝按国家行为者区分威胁级别感到失望。他说：“他们把以一种非常不同的方式运作的演员混为一谈，可能是为了让这听起来更像是两党合作。”“我就是不明白为什么。”
微软表示，在过去的一年里，它观察到Fancy Bear试图侵入与美国大选直接和间接相关的人的账户，其中包括为共和党和民主党竞选活动以及国家和州政党组织提供服务的顾问-总共有200多个团体。
欧洲议会中最大的团体-中间偏右的欧洲人民党(European People‘s Party)也受到了攻击。该党发言人表示，黑客企图未获成功。美国智库德国马歇尔基金会是另一个目标。一位发言人表示，没有证据表明有人入侵。
微软没有说明俄罗斯黑客是否曾试图侵入拜登的竞选活动，但确实表示，来自政府支持的“熊猫飓风”组织的中国黑客“似乎间接地、但没有成功地”通过拜登竞选团队下属的非竞选电子邮件账户攻击拜登竞选活动。
拜登竞选团队没有证实这一企图，尽管他们在一份声明中表示，他们知道微软的报告。
这篇博客文章称，伊朗政府支持的黑客曾在今年5月和6月试图登录特朗普竞选团队和政府官员的账户，但均未成功。特朗普竞选团队副新闻秘书Thea McDonald称：“我们是一个很大的目标，所以看到针对竞选团队或我们工作人员的恶意活动并不令人意外。”她拒绝进一步置评。
今年6月，谷歌披露，飓风熊猫以特朗普竞选团队工作人员为目标，而伊朗黑客试图侵入拜登竞选工作人员的账户。这类网络钓鱼尝试通常涉及伪造电子邮件，这些电子邮件带有旨在获取密码或用恶意软件感染设备的链接。
虽然两人都是阿蒂。威廉·巴尔将军和国家安全顾问罗伯特·奥布莱恩都表示，中国是美国选举的最大威胁，唯一提到的特朗普政府官员被中国黑客攻击的是“至少一名曾与特朗普政府有关联的知名人士”。
大西洋理事会数字取证研究主任格雷厄姆·布鲁基(Graham Brookie)驳斥了巴尔和奥布莱恩关于中国对今年大选构成更大威胁的说法。他的实验室处于挖掘和宣传俄罗斯造谣运动的最前线。
布鲁基证实，他的雇主是熊猫飓风的目标之一，但他表示，没有证据表明黑客企图与2020年大选有关。他表示，黑客企图没有成功。
他说：“我们有充分的迹象表明，这是一起网络间谍活动，收集信息，而不是干预选举。”
相比之下，Brookie说，“很明显，俄罗斯的尝试(微软披露的)集中在选举过程和致力于此的团体上。”
note_en:
The same Russian military intelligence outfit that hacked the Democrats in 2016 has attempted similar intrusions into the computer systems of more than 200 organizations, including political parties and consultants, Microsoft said Thursday.
 Those efforts appear to be part of a broader increase in targeting of U.S. political campaigns and related groups, the company said. “What we’ve seen is consistent with previous attack patterns that not only target candidates and campaign staffers but also those who they consult on key issues,” Tom Burt, a Microsoft vice president, said in a blog post.
 Most of the infiltration attempts by Russian, Chinese and Iranian agents were halted by Microsoft security software and the targets notified, he said. The company would not comment on who may have been successfully hacked or the effect.
 Microsoft did not assess which foreign adversary poses the greater threat to the integrity of the November presidential election. The consensus among cybersecurity experts is that Russian interference is the gravest. Senior Trump administration officials have disputed that, though without offering any evidence.
  “This is the actor from 2016, potentially conducting business as usual,” said John Hultquist, director of intelligence analysis at the top cybersecurity company FireEye. “We believe that Russian military intelligence continues to pose the greatest threat to the democratic process.”
 The Microsoft post shows that Russian military intelligence continues to pursue election-related targets undeterred by U.S. indictments, sanctions and other countermeasures, Hultquist said. It interfered in the 2016 campaign seeking to benefit the Trump campaign by hacking the Democratic National Committee and emails of John Podesta, the campaign manager of Hillary Clinton, and dumping embarrassing material online, congressional and FBI investigators have found.
 The same GRU military intelligence unit, known as Fancy Bear, that Microsoft identifies as being behind the current election-related activity also broke into voter registration databases in at least three states in 2016, though there is no evidence it tried to interfere with voting.
 Microsoft, which has visibility into these efforts because its software is both ubiquitous and highly rated for security, did not address whether U.S. officials who manage elections or operate voting systems have been targeted by state-backed hackers this year. U.S. intelligence officials say they have so far not seen evidence of that. They said last month that the  Russians favor President Trump and that the Chinese prefer former Vice President Joe Biden, the Democratic challenger.
     In a  Washington Post opinion piece this week, Susan Gordon, the deputy director of national intelligence from 2017 to 2019, said that “Russia’s intent is to undermine American democracy” while China seeks, by contrast, to shape U.S. policy and erode U.S. global influence, in part by the theft of intellectual property.
 Thomas Rid, a Johns Hopkins geopolitics expert, said he was disappointed by Microsoft’s refusal to differentiate threat level by state actor. “They’re lumping in actors that operate in a very different fashion, probably to make this sound more bipartisan,” he said. “I just don’t understand why.”
 Microsoft said in the last year it has observed attempts by Fancy Bear to break into the accounts of people directly and indirectly affiliated with the U.S. election, including consultants serving Republican and Democratic campaigns and national and state party organizations — more than 200 groups in all.
  Also targeted was the center-right European People’s Party, the largest grouping in the European Parliament. A party spokesperson said the hacking attempts were unsuccessful. The German Marshall Fund of the United States, a think tank, was another target. A spokesperson said there was no evidence of intrusion.
 Microsoft did not say whether Russian hackers had attempted to break into the Biden campaign but did say that Chinese hackers from the state-backed group known as Hurricane Panda “appears to have indirectly and unsuccessfully” targeted the Biden campaign through non-campaign email accounts belonging to people affiliated with it.
 The Biden campaign did not confirm the attempt, although it said in a statement that it was aware of the Microsoft report.
 The blog post said Iranian state-backed hackers have unsuccessfully tried to log into accounts of Trump campaign and administration officials in May and June of this year. “We are a large target, so it is not surprising to see malicious activity directed at the campaign or our staff,” Trump campaign deputy press secretary Thea McDonald said. She declined to comment further.
  In June, Google disclosed that Hurricane Panda had targeted Trump campaign staffers while Iranian hackers had attempted to breach accounts of Biden campaign workers. Such phishing attempts typically involve forged emails with links designed to harvest passwords or infect devices with malware.
 Although both Atty. Gen. William Barr and National Security Advisor Robert O’Brien have both said China represents the greatest threat to U.S. elections, the only mention of a Trump administration official targeted by Chinese hackers is “at least one prominent individual formerly associated with” the administration.
 Graham Brookie, director of digital forensic research at the Atlantic Council, disputes Barr‘s and O’Brien’s claim that China poses the greater threat to this year’s election. His lab is at the forefront of unearthing and publicizing Russian disinformation campaigns.
 Brookie confirmed that his employer was among targets of Hurricane Panda but said there was no evidence the hacking attempts, which he said were unsuccessful, had anything to do with the 2020 election.
  “We have every indication that this was an instance of cyberespionage, information gathering, as opposed to electoral interference,” he said.
 By contrast, Brookie said, “It’s pretty evident that the Russian attempts (that Microsoft disclosed) were focused on electoral processes and groups working on that.”

根据公司的规格，Tarform的第一款街头合法进入者露娜起价24,000美元，时速从0到60英里只需3.8秒，城市续航里程为120英里，最高时速为120英里，50分钟内充电至80%。
这款车型是该公司的使命，将美学设计和工艺与两轮电动汽车的环境可持续性融合在一起。
为此，露娜融入了许多独特的生态设计特征。车身由亚麻籽编织而成，整个摩托车工程避免使用塑料。露娜的座椅软垫是用可生物降解的纯素皮革制成的。Tarform还在测试避免在摩托车上涂油漆和底漆的方法，取而代之的是使用注入藻类和铁基金属颜料的单一材料。
该公司由瑞典人塔拉斯·克拉夫楚克(Taras Kravtchouk)创立-他是一名工业设计专家，曾是初创公司的负责人，也是一名热情的摩托车司机。露娜的发布紧随2018年两款概念电动车的首次亮相。
在谈到Tarform的目标市场时，他解释说，这家初创公司希望吸引那些可能对过去50年来促使人们骑摩托车的东西感到厌烦的人-即汽油、铬、噪音和烟雾。
Kravtchouk告诉TechCrunch：“这更多的是为那些想要定制自行车的人和技术人员准备的：他们想要一辆摩托车，但不想与整个被污名化的摩托车生活方式联系在一起。”
Tarform进入电动汽车领域的竞争来自几家电动摩托车初创公司-以及OEM-这些公司正试图将汽油骑手转变为电动车手，并吸引年轻一代骑摩托车。
领头羊之一是加利福尼亚州的Zero摩托车公司，在全球拥有200家经销商。Zero在2019年推出了一款售价1.9万美元的SR/F，城市续航里程161英里，一小时充电能力，最高时速124英里。意大利Energica公司正在扩大其高性能电动摩托车在美国的分销。
2020年，哈雷·戴维森(Harley Davidson)成为第一家在美国提供街头合法电动摩托车的大型天然气制造商，售价2.9万美元的LiveWire。
加拿大初创公司Damon Motors今年首次推出了时速200英里、售价2.4万美元的Hypersport，它提供了专有的安全和人体工程学技术，可以调整骑行位置和盲点检测。
在谈到Tarform计划如何与这些电动摩托车制造商竞争时，Kravtchouk解释说，这不是该公司的优先事项。“我们甚至还没有接近Zero或其他大公司的产量，但这不是我们的意图。把(露娜)看作是定制生产的自行车，“他说。
Kravtchouk补充说：“我们并没有打算制造一辆速度最快或续航里程最长的自行车。”“我们着手制造一款完全修正电动摩托车制造和供应链的自行车，以一种符合道德的方式采购我们的材料，并创建一条符合道德的供应链。”
对于这项任务，Tarform已经从几个家族理财室和天使投资者那里获得了资金，其中包括洛杉矶的M13。据首席执行官塔拉斯·克拉夫楚克表示，这家总部位于布鲁克林的电动摩托车公司正在接受新款露娜的预购，并寻求在2021年进行首轮融资。
note_en:
Tarform’s first street legal entrant, the Luna, starts at $24,000, does 0-60 mph in 3.8 seconds, has a city range of 120 miles, top-speed of 120 mph, and charges to 80% in 50 minutes—according to company specs.
 The model was hatched out of the company’s mission to meld aesthetic design and craftsmanship to environmental sustainability in two-wheeled electric vehicles.
  To that end, the Luna incorporates a number of unique, eco-design features. The bodywork is made from a flax seed weave and the overall motorcycle engineering avoids use of plastics. The Luna’s seat upholstery is made out of biodegradable vegan leather. Tarform is also testing methods to avoid paints and primers on its motorcycles, instead using a mono-material infused with algae and iron based metallic pigments.
 The company was founded by Swede Taras Kravtchouk—an industrial design specialist, former startup head, and passionate motorcyclist. The Luna launch follows the  debut of two concept e-motos in 2018.
  On Tarform’s target market, he explained the startup hopes to attract those who may be turned off by the very things that have turned people on to motorcycling over the last 50 years—namely gas, chrome, noise, and fumes.
 “It’s more for people who want a custom bike and the techies: people who wanted to have a motorcycle but didn’t want to be associated with the whole stigmatized motorcycle lifestyle,” Kravtchouk told TechCrunch.
 Tarform enters the EV arena with competition from  several e-moto startups—and on OEM—that are attempting to convert gas riders to electric and attract a younger generation to motorcycling.
 One of the leaders is California company  Zero Motorcycles, with 200 dealers worldwide. Zero introduced a its $19,000 SR/F in 2019, with a 161-mile city range, one-hour charge capability and a top speed of 124 mph.  Italy’s Energica is expanding distribution of its high-performance e-motos in the U.S.
 In 2020, Harley Davidson became the first of the big gas manufacturers to offer a street-legal e-motorcycle for sale in the U.S., the $29,000 LiveWire.
 And Canadian startup  Damon Motors debuted its 200 mph, $24,000 Hypersport this year, which offers proprietary safety and ergonomics tech for adjustable riding positions and blind-spot detection.
 On how Tarform plans to compete with these e-motorcycle players, Kravtchouk explained that’s not the company’s priority. “We’re not even close in production to Zero or the other big guys, but that’s not our intention. Think of the [Luna] as a custom production bike,” he said.
  “We did not set out to build a bike that is fastest or has the longest range,” Kravtchouk added. “We set out to build a bike that completely revises the manufacturing and supply chain of e-motorcycles in a way where we ethically source our materials and create an ethical supply-chain.”
 For this mission, Tarform has obtained funding from several family offices and angel investors, including  LA based M13. The Brooklyn based e-motorcycle company is taking pre-orders on its new Luna and pursuing a Series-A funding round for 2021, according to CEO Taras Kravtchouk.

(美国有线电视新闻网)2017年1月12日，当消防员到达奥斯汀·帕拉的家时，他们可以看到外面的烟和烧焦的办公椅残留物。
当时20岁的帕拉已经被送往医院。他的母亲向消防员解释说，她儿子的椅子在他睡觉时着火了，当他把燃烧的椅子搬到外面时被烧伤了。
负责调查这起事件的康涅狄格州韦瑟斯菲尔德消防局局长安东尼·迪格诺蒂(Anthony Dignoti)可以看到，门和门框也被大火烧毁了。他注意到到处都是碗，他在官方报告中写道，碗里装满了水，试图扑灭大火。
但最让迪格诺蒂感兴趣的是一根白色的USB线。他在报道和接受CNN采访时说，绳子的一部分挂在椅子上，仍然完好无损，但另一边粘在座位上，已经熔化成一根裸露的电线。
迪格诺蒂最终得出结论，火灾是由帕拉用来给手机充电的电线引起的。他的报告说，电线发生了短路，虽然不清楚为什么会发生这种情况，但电线产生的热量点燃了办公椅的软垫。
电源线上印有世界上最大的在线零售商的名字：亚马逊。
它是由亚马逊颇受欢迎的自有品牌之一AmazonBasics销售的，该公司提供价格实惠的产品，包括消费电子产品、家用电器、家居用品和办公配件。
据零售商称，亚马逊基础(AmazonBasics)于2009年推出，目前已提供5000多种产品。它的使命是：识别亚马逊可以创造出与现有名牌产品类似或更高质量、更低价位的日常商品-这一策略也被好市多(Costco)和塔吉特(Target)等公司采用。
使用包括#34；FIRE；&34；危险#34；和#34；BURN#34在内的关键词，记者发现，从2016年到2020年初，美国客户在亚马逊网站上发布了1500多条关于亚马逊基础电子产品和家电的评论，这些评论描述了与安全相关的问题。
美国有线电视新闻网(CNN)确认的评论中，超过10%的人报告说，产品着火了--在某些情况下，描述了火焰是如何从设备中喷出的。
近200人包括房屋或财物受损的报告，包括烧焦的墙壁和烧焦的手机。
根据Marketplace Pulse的研究，自该系列推出以来，越来越多的亚马逊基础产品(AmazonBasics)在其网站上大力推广，成为畅销产品，许多产品的评级都在4星以上。近几个月来，这家在线零售商的销售额一直在飙升，因为在疫情持续期间，数以百万计的美国人一直呆在家里--在许多情况下，还在远程工作。
但消费者在对政府监管机构的投诉和亚马逊自己网站上发布的评论中，对亚马逊基础商品提出了严重的安全担忧。根据亚马逊网站上列出的一项对亚马逊电子和家电的分析，自2016年以来，至少有1500条评论描述了产品爆炸、起火、吸烟、熔化、导致电气故障或其他构成风险的情况，涉及70多个项目。
确定的评论只占产品总购买量的一小部分，消费电子产品引发的火灾并不是亚马逊品牌商品独有的。用户错误也可能是一个因素，家庭内的布线故障或老化，或者与产品结合使用的有缺陷的设备也可能是一个因素。
但CNN采访的电气工程师表示，当消费者制作精良并正确使用时，像亚马逊基础(AmazonBasics)品牌销售的电子产品应该很少会构成危险。
在1500多条评论中，许多消费者明确指出产品存在潜在危险--使用诸如危险或火灾之类的术语，或者说产品应该被召回。今天，亚马逊上仍有大约30件有三条或更多这样的评论的商品出售。在发表时，至少有11种符合这一标准的其他产品不再销售。一些产品在CNN开始报道后就无法使用了，至少有四个产品页面从这家零售商的网站上被完全删除，留下了被员工称为狗页面的死URL。亚马逊证实，其中至少有八个产品正在接受调查，但表示该公司确定它们都符合其安全标准。
顾客在评论中写道，并在采访中表示，他们相信亚马逊基础(AmazonBasics)的购买将是安全和优质的，因为它们被贴上了亚马逊的品牌，并经常被吹捧为亚马逊的选择。但即使投诉不断增加，该公司也很少或根本没有向消费者或公众提供有关其如何处理有关其一些商品不安全的指控的信息。
亚马逊的购物者生动地讲述了可怕的故障和死里逃生的细节：一个电涌保护器变成了一个喷灯，一位父亲回忆说，火焰从他婴儿托儿所附近的设备里喷了出来。据说手机充电器烫伤了人们的手和腿，爆炸的电池据称还向某人的脸上喷洒了化学物质。据一位家长称，一辆停放的汽车里，一条USB线在一个蹒跚学步的孩子在车内时突然起火。据报道，另一辆车的充电器在高速公路上引发电气火灾，据称烧毁了司机和一件夹克。据多名消费者称，碎纸机是自己打开的，据报道，其中一台炸毁了一个火球，烧毁了某人的手臂，烧焦了头发。一位母亲声称，当一名8岁的女孩像她无数次一样去加热她的通心粉芝士杯时，一台微波炉突然着火了，她说她必须把电器拿到外面用软管喷洒。其中每一笔购买都经过了验证，这意味着亚马逊证实，撰写评论的顾客确实是在网站上购买了产品，根据亚马逊网站的说法，他没有获得大幅折扣。有几个还附有烧毁物品的照片。
虽然确定故障原因的最佳方法是对其进行物理测试并将其拆开，但许多客户表示，应亚马逊的要求，他们会立即扔掉有缺陷的设备或将其退还给亚马逊。
CNN从客户那里获得了两款损坏的AmazonBasics产品：一台微波炉着火了，一名用户说一根USB线过热融化了。这些测试是由马里兰大学高级生命周期工程中心(CALCE)故障分析实验室的研究人员应CNN的要求进行的。
USB线烧得太厉害，研究人员无法确定出了什么问题。微波测试发现，覆盖微波炉内加热设备的面板的设计可能会导致机器着火，并确定面板的固定方式可能会允许食物或油脂等碎片聚集在后面，并可能着火。研究人员一打开微波炉，它就开始产生火花和冒烟，导致它做出反应，就像它的使用者在里面放了铝箔或其他金属一样。由于新冠肺炎的原因，实验室关闭了，测试被缩短了。
CALCE的创始人、工程学教授迈克尔·佩赫特(Michael Pecht)表示，使用这台机器肯定会有风险，而且它是一个安全风险，因为这显然会升温到可能发生火灾的程度。他之前曾协助政府进行安全调查。这不仅仅是一个可靠性问题，这是一个潜在的安全问题。
亚马逊没有评论微波炉是否有任何改进，但表示有信心微波炉可以安全使用，它将继续满足或超过所有适用的认证要求。
该零售商表示，安全是公司的头等大事，它采取了一系列措施来确保所有AmazonBasics产品都是安全和高质量的，比如选择经验丰富的制造商，监控客户反馈和测试项目，以确保它们在上市前后都通过了安全和合规标准。它还表示，AmazonBasics提供了数千种产品，这些产品总共有100多万条评论，对这些担忧进行了彻底的调查，公司也采取了相应的行动。
该公司发言人在一份声明中表示，调查结果因个案而异，可能包括将产品从商店下架，调整产品设计，通知顾客停止使用该产品，或采取其他适当行动。我们希望顾客信心十足地购物，如果顾客有任何顾虑，他们可以联系客服，我们会进行调查。
亚马逊表示，商品可能不再可用的原因有很多，但如果发现关键的安全问题，客户将得到通知。当被问及该公司多久一次这样做时，亚马逊表示，它通知客户有关AmazonBasics产品的次数不到五次。它没有具体说明是否对CNN审查的任何项目这样做。
亚马逊已经因为允许拥有据称危险产品的第三方卖家在该网站上做生意而受到严格审查，多项法院裁决发现，该零售商可能会对在其第三方市场销售的有缺陷的商品承担责任。
CNN的分析集中在以亚马逊自己的名字出售的产品上，亚马逊在该零售商的业务中所占的比重越来越大。
这些评论来自生活在美国各地的人们，时间跨度为五年，但它们往往会唤起人们对同样问题的关注：
微波炉内的同一面板着火，USB线在没有明显磨损或过度使用的情况下融化或燃烧，户外庭院加热器上的油漆着火。消费者声称，他们第一次插入物品时就出现了故障。其他人说，当电子产品开始出现故障时，它们并不在使用中。
多名电气工程师表示，通常情况下，一两份问题报告可能更容易被归因于用户错误或其他外部因素。但是，随着关于同一项目的同类故障报告的数量增加，设计或制造中存在缺陷的可能性也在增加。
波士顿大学工程学院(Boston University‘s College of Engineering)教授马克·霍伦斯坦(Mark Horenstein)表示，这肯定会导致更多人怀疑产品有问题。这是一个危险信号。
亚马逊表示，客户评论只是潜在问题的一个迹象，称在评估潜在问题时，它会考虑销售历史、退货和客服联系等其他一些因素。该公司在一份声明中表示，仅凭顾客评论就得出产品不安全的结论，或者暗示这是一个普遍存在的问题，这是误导性的。
亚马逊的前员工说，即使有几条评论提到了像“火”和“危险”这样的字眼，也会自动促使零售商采取行动。亚马逊表示，审查受到监控，可能引发安全调查，但拒绝提供发生这种情况所需的具体门槛的细节。该公司表示，在这样的询问期间，产品可能会被暂时移除，为了继续销售一些东西，必须确认它是安全的。该公司还表示，如果调查发现潜在的、非孤立的安全问题，它将采取适当措施通知政府，并安全召回该产品。
法律要求企业立即向联邦消费品安全委员会(CPSC)报告具有潜在危险的物品，以便该机构确定是否有必要正式召回。公司也可以与CPSC合作发起自愿召回。
与亚马逊评论中详细描述的担忧类似，至少有10份报告特别提到了AmazonBasics的产品，并向CPSC传达了这些担忧。这些投诉至少涉及8个不同的项目，可以追溯到2012年。
在美国，亚马逊在2018年和2019年公开召回了两款AmazonBasics产品，此前该公司在美国收到了53份关于电力银行过热的报告，以及25份关于空间加热器过热、燃烧或火花的报告。该公司表示，它主动通知了CPSC该公司自己的调查结果，并打算召回这些产品。
除了这两次官方召回，该公司从未公开承认AmazonBasics产品存在任何安全问题。
CPSC表示，法律禁止讨论任何未被召回的产品，一般来说，该机构根据一系列因素来确定是否有必要召回，这些因素包括缺陷的性质、与问题相关的危险程度以及类似问题的模式(见图)。
然而，该零售商确实推出了新版本的产品，称其进行了更新，以改善客户体验。
马特·西特罗(Matt Citro)购买了他的AmazonBasics浪涌保护器，以保护家人免受火灾的伤害。相反，他说，2018年1月，浪涌保护器本身起火了。只有一个手机充电器插在设备上，但当时没有使用。
当他9个月大的儿子在附近的托儿所睡觉时，他坐在沙发上，他说他注意到电涌保护器里冒出火焰--把它变成了一个类似喷灯的东西。他告诉CNN，他很快就把燃烧的装置从墙上拉了出来。他没有受伤，但他说，电涌保护器在墙上插座上烧了一个洞，烧焦了他的部分墙壁，给他留下了超过1000美元的损失。
他说，在此之前，他的家里从未遇到过任何电气问题，他相信AmazonBasics电涌保护器是罪魁祸首。
雪铁龙在一篇评论中写道：不要买这款产品！……如果我不在家，我的整个房子都会被这款便宜的产品烧毁。我对亚马逊非常失望。我们对他们的产品很有信心，(其中一个)几乎烧毁了我的家，这让我不信任他们。这个产品上面有亚马逊的名字！&#34；
雪铁龙说，他立即联系了亚马逊，并告诉该公司发生了什么。起初，他说有人向他提供更换或退款。他不满意，继续打电话给客服。
他说，根据CNN查看的一份和解文件，他终于接通了为他与一家保险公司牵线搭桥的人，最终他获得了1469美元的赔偿。亚马逊在该文件中否认负有任何责任。
在雪铁龙发布他的评论后，亚马逊继续销售浪涌保护器近两年，在此期间，更多关于类似情况和其他担忧的评论堆积如山。40多名顾客报告说，该产品存在火灾危险，对他们的家或财物造成了损害，或描述了其他危险。
截至去年年底，在亚马逊将这款售价10.99美元的设备从网站上删除之前，美国发布了大约2600条评论，这些评论约占总数的1.7%。几条评论中包括雪铁龙(Citro；s)等着火和起火的说法。相比之下，今年早些时候，一家大型消费电子公司生产的、也在亚马逊网站上销售的一款类似产品收到了6条关于可能存在的安全问题的评论，占其8000多条评论的0.07%。六个人都没有提到真正的火灾。亚马逊说，它自己的分析增加了对AmazonBasics电涌保护器的全球评论，发现1.1%涉及过热、火灾和其他危险的说法。
亚马逊基础(AmazonBasics)的一名前产品经理表示，听到如此高的评论比例引发了亚马逊基础产品的安全问题，她感到惊讶。由于仍在该行业工作，这名经理要求匿名。她在谈到CNN发现的评论时说，一旦你得到40分，哦，天哪，不，这在任何形式、方式或形式上都是不可接受的。她补充说，当她在那里工作时，0.05%左右的比率会被认为是更容易接受的。但她为她的前雇主辩护，称这只是一种产品，在她为公司工作期间，她认为这家零售商在试图对安全问题做出反应方面甚至比竞争对手更警惕。
在CNN开始报道Protec增兵几周后。
